forecast-period.text: A low pressure system is expected to move slowly eastwards over waters to the south of the state and will extend a trough over southern parts of SA later today and Monday. The low will then move over Bass Strait later Monday. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots turning southwesterly in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres.
South to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1 to 2 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the evening.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 10 to 15 knots in the late evening.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre later in the evening.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 2 metres during the evening.
Southerly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the evening.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 25 knots in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm this morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Southeast to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Sunday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots in the west during the morning.
2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres in the west.
Westerly 5 to 6 metres, tending southwesterly 4 to 6 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming west to southwesterly about 10 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Southerly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Sunday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 35 knots turning westerly 25 to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
2.5 to 4 metres.
Westerly 4 to 6 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the early morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots tending westerly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres south of Salt Creek.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds tending west to southwesterly early in the morning.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2.5 to 3 metres, tending southwesterly 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming westerly below 10 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Sunday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots offshore in the afternoon.
2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 5 metres south of Beachport around midday.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres inshore, increasing to 3 to 5 metres offshore.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning west to southwesterly 20 to 30 knots early in the morning.
2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres south of Beachport.
Westerly 2.5 to 4 metres, tending southwesterly 3 to 4 metres around midday.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots turning westerly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the day. Winds turning westerly in the early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers south of Cowell to Wallaroo, 50% chance elsewhere.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Westerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo early in the morning. Winds turning southwesterly in the late morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming variable about 10 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Port Vincent to Brighton around midday.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Port Vincent to Brighton during the morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning west to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable below 10 knots during the evening.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning westerly in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 4 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
West to southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the day.
Around 1 metre, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Partly cloudy.
